evidence_items.title: Skills assessment from relevant assessing body
evidence_items.description: A positive skills assessment from the relevant occupational assessing authority confirming the applicant's qualifications and experience are suitable for their nominated occupation.

evidence_items.title: Invitation to Apply (ITA) from SkillSelect
evidence_items.description: An Invitation to Apply for the Subclass 189 visa received through SkillSelect based on the applicant's points score.

evidence_items.title: English language test result
evidence_items.description: An official English test result demonstrating the required level of English proficiency for the points score claimed.
examples: IELTS Academic — minimum 6.0 each band for 10 points, 7.0 for 20 points

evidence_items.title: Employment history evidence
evidence_items.description: Documentation of skilled employment in the nominated occupation used to claim experience points.

evidence_items.title: Academic qualifications
evidence_items.description: Degree certificates and transcripts supporting the qualifications claimed in the EOI and points test.

evidence_items.title: Health examination
evidence_items.description: Completed health examination conducted by an approved panel physician, submitted to Home Affairs via the HAP system.

evidence_items.title: Police clearance certificate
evidence_items.description: Police clearance certificates from Australia and all countries where the applicant has lived for 12 months or more in the past 10 years.
examples: Australian Federal Police (AFP) national police check

requirements.title: Valid invitation to apply
requirements.description: The applicant must have received a valid Invitation to Apply (ITA) through SkillSelect.

requirements.title: Positive skills assessment
requirements.description: The applicant must hold a positive skills assessment from the relevant authority for their nominated occupation.

requirements.title: Minimum points score
requirements.description: The applicant must have a points score of at least 65 (the minimum threshold) at the time of invitation.

requirements.title: English language proficiency
requirements.description: The applicant must demonstrate at least Competent English (minimum IELTS 6.0 in each band or equivalent).

requirements.title: Health requirement
requirements.description: The applicant and any dependants must meet the health requirement.

requirements.title: Character requirement
requirements.description: The applicant must meet the character requirements under Section 501 of the Migration Act.

flags.title: Points claimed may exceed what evidence supports
flags.why_it_matters: Points claimed in your EOI must be supported by evidence at the time of invitation and application. Claiming points you cannot evidence — for experience, qualifications, or English — can lead to refusal.
actions: Recalculate your points against the official points test schedule
Confirm each point claim is backed by a document
Pay particular attention to overseas employment — reference letters must clearly state dates, role, and hours
evidence_examples: Detailed reference letters from all employers
English test result matching the points band claimed
Degree certificates matching qualification points claimed

flags.title: Skills assessment may be invalid or expired
flags.why_it_matters: Skills assessments have validity periods (typically 3 years). An expired or invalid assessment means the core eligibility requirement cannot be met.
actions: Check the validity date on your assessment outcome letter
Apply for a reassessment if your assessment has expired
Confirm the assessing body is correct for your occupation and visa subclass
evidence_examples: Skills assessment outcome letter (showing validity period)

flags.title: Employment history has unexplained gaps
flags.why_it_matters: Gaps in skilled employment can reduce points claims and raise questions about continuous skilled work experience.
actions: Account for all gaps with a written explanation
Document any periods of study, self-employment, or travel
Ensure payslips and reference letters cover the full claimed employment period without gaps
evidence_examples: Study or travel records for gap periods

flags.title: English test result may be expired
flags.why_it_matters: English test results must be valid (typically within 3 years of the test date) at the time of application. An expired test means points cannot be claimed for English proficiency.
actions: Check the expiry date of your English test result
Book a resit if your result has or will expire before you expect to lodge
Consider sitting a higher-band test to maximise English points while eligible
evidence_examples: Current IELTS / PTE / TOEFL score report within validity period
